mission.title: alertmanager: alertmanager-config.yaml file for pagerduty_configs.details does not respect EventApi v2 spec
mission.description: alertmanager-config.yaml file for pagerduty_configs.details does not respect EventApi v2 spec. Requested by 11+ users.

steps.title: Check current alertmanager deployment
steps.description:
Verify your alertmanager version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager
helm list -n alertmanager 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working alertmanager installation.

steps.title: Review alertmanager configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant alertmanager configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager
kubectl get configmap -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=alertmanager
```
**What did you do?**
Generate a nested object for pagerduty_configs.details field inside alertmanager-config.yaml

**What did you expect to see?**
amtool should not fail and alertmanager must allow nested objects as it's compatible with pagerduty

steps.title: Apply the fix for alertmanager-config.yaml file for pagerduty_configs.details…
steps.description:
This change allows nested key/value pair in pageduty configuration.

Events API V1 supports an object in `details` field:
https://developer.pagerduty.com/docs/send-v1-event#parameters

Events API V2 supports an object in `payload.custom_details`
```yaml
global:
  resolve_timeout: "10m"

templates:
  - '/etc/alertmanager/secrets/pagerduty.integration.key.tmpl'

route:
  group_by: [ '...' ]
  group_wait: 30s
  group_interval: 5m
  repeat_interval: 12h
  receiver: 'black-hole'

  routes:
    - match_re:
        severity: "warning|critical"
      receiver: "all"

inhibit_rules:
  - source_match:
      severity: 'critical'
    target_match:
      severity: 'warning'
    equal: [ 'alertname' ]

receivers:
  - name: "all"
    pagerduty_configs:
      - routing_key: '{{ template "pagerduty.integration.key" . }}'
        details:
          firing:    
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager
kubectl get events -n alertmanager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "alertmanager-config.yaml file for pagerduty_configs.details…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: This change allows nested key/value pair in pageduty configuration.
codeSnippets:

  resolve_timeout: "10m"

templates:

inhibit_rules:
  - source_match:
      severity: 'critical'
    target_match:
      severity: 'warning'

receivers:
  - name: "all"
    pagerduty_configs:
      - routing_key: '{{ template "pagerduty.integration.key" . }}'
        details:
          firing:       '{{ template "pagerduty.default.instances" .Alerts.Firing }}'
          resolved:     '{{ template "pagerduty.default.instances" .Alerts.Resolved }}'
          num_firing:   '{{ .Alerts.Firing | le

level=error ts=2021-02-05T10:57:07.101Z caller=coordinator.go:124 component=configuration msg="Loading configuration file failed" file=/etc/alertmanager/config/
alertmanager.yaml err="yaml: unmarshal errors:\n  line 91: cannot unmarshal !!seq into string"

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with alertmanager installed or the issue environment reproducible.
